channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables efficient on-device function calling for tasks like calendar management and game logic, enhancing mobile AI capabilities. This development allows complex tasks to be performed offline with low latency, marking a significant step in mobile AI technology.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports various third-party tools and integrations, enabling developers to connect their AI agents with platforms like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This annual event aims to engage participants and highlight advancements in AI technology leading up to the main conference.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring technical tracks on topics like Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person experiences for developers navigating the transition to agentic AI.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers improved GPU and NPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance the efficiency of deploying GenAI and on-device inference, solidifying LiteRT's role in the TensorFlow ecosystem.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly session designed to help developers improve their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative focuses on practical applications and aims to provide immediate benefits to participants' workflows.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to transform AI into a more tailored collaborator for developers.

item.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
item.description: Zero Shot, a new venture capital fund with ties to OpenAI, is raising $100 million for its first fund and has already begun investing. This initiative reflects the growing interest in AI-driven startups and technologies.

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google has quietly launched an offline-first dictation app powered by Gemma AI models, aiming to compete with existing dictation tools like Wispr Flow. This app enhances accessibility and functionality for users without constant internet access.

item.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
item.description: Iran has threatened to target U.S.-linked data centers with missile strikes amid escalating tensions between the two nations. This threat raises concerns about the security of data infrastructure in the context of geopolitical conflicts.

item.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
item.description: OpenAI suggests implementing taxes on AI profits and creating public wealth funds to mitigate job loss and inequality, aiming to reshape the economic landscape as AI evolves.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications open: a chance for VC access, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
item.description: Applications are now open for Startup Battlefield 200, offering startups a chance for venture capital access, TechCrunch coverage, and a $100K prize.

item.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
item.description: The new ChatGPT app integrations allow users to interact with services like DoorDash and Spotify directly within the ChatGPT interface, enhancing user experience.

item.title: Ticket savings of up to $500 this week for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: TechCrunch is offering nearly $500 off tickets for Disrupt 2026, available for a limited time, encouraging early registration.

item.title: Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI
item.description: Spain's Xoople has raised $130 million in Series B funding to develop Earth-mapping technology for AI, alongside a partnership with L3Harris for spacecraft sensors.

item.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
item.description: Iran's IRGC has threatened OpenAI's planned data center in Abu Dhabi in response to U.S. military threats, escalating geopolitical tensions surrounding AI infrastructure.

item.title: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
item.description: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins discusses the potential for data centers in space, emphasizing the future of technology and data management beyond Earth.

item.title: Inner Cosmologies of Enclosed Landscapes
item.description: This article explores the concept of enclosed landscapes in literature, discussing their protective and aesthetic functions in various settings.

item.title: wrightty 0.1.0
item.description: The wrightty 0.1.0 release introduces a Python SDK for the Wrightty terminal automation protocol, enhancing automation capabilities.

item.title: moxing 0.1.26
item.description: The moxing 0.1.26 release provides a Python wrapper for llama.cpp, compatible with OpenAI's API and featuring auto GPU detection.

item.title: mdarena added to PyPI
item.description: mdarena has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), allowing users to benchmark their CLAUDE.md against their own pull requests. This tool enhances the evaluation process for developers.

item.title: ntlabs 1.7.0
item.description: The Neural Thinkers LAB AI Platform has released version 1.7.0 of its Python SDK, introducing features like AI Agents and OAuth v2 SSO. This update aims to improve user experience and functionality for developers.

item.title: claude-agent-bridge 0.4.2
item.description: The claude-agent-bridge 0.4.2 allows for multi-session Claude Code dispatch via Telegram. This update facilitates better communication and task management for users.

item.title: Killer robots are here. Now what? (Lock and Code S07E07)
item.description: In the latest Lock and Code podcast, Peter Asaro discusses the implications of killer robots and potential measures to mitigate their risks. This conversation highlights the ethical concerns surrounding AI in warfare.

item.title: Inference Costs Are Not Sustainable
item.description: A report discusses the unsustainable costs associated with AI inference, highlighting the rapid consumption of subscription resources. This raises concerns about the economic viability of current AI models.

item.title: agentic-programming added to PyPI
item.description: Agentic Programming has been added to PyPI, offering Python functions that automatically track context when calling LLMs. This feature aims to streamline the programming process for developers.

item.title: torchfoo 0.0.3
item.description: torchfoo 0.0.3 has been released, providing a collection of general utilities for PyTorch. This update aims to assist developers in their machine learning projects.

item.title: OpenAI Operations Chief Changes Jobs Amid IPO Preparations
item.description: OpenAI's chief operating officer, Brad Lightcap, is reportedly transitioning to new responsibilities amid a leadership overhaul as the company prepares for an IPO. This change signals a shift in strategic focus.

item.title: Bold promises vs. hard proof: Indian IT faces an AI test this fiscal
item.description: Indian IT companies are under pressure to deliver on AI efficiency promises made after the rise of ChatGPT. The upcoming fiscal year will test their commitments to clients.

item.title: langgraph-otel-topology-instrumentor added to PyPI
item.description: The langgraph-otel-topology-instrumentor has been added to PyPI, providing OpenTelemetry instrumentation for LangGraph. This tool captures graph topology and state changes, aiding developers in monitoring.

item.title: fastapi-fullstack 0.2.3
item.description: FastAPI has released a new template generator that integrates with Next.js, PydanticAI, and LangChain, enabling developers to quickly build AI applications. This tool includes over 20 enterprise integrations and observability features.

item.title: I used Claude Code, Antigravity, and Perplexity Computer to build a portfolio — there was a clear winner
item.description: A user compared Claude Code, Antigravity, and Perplexity Computer for building a portfolio, revealing unexpected results in performance and usability. The findings highlight the varying capabilities of these AI tools.

item.title: evalora added to PyPI
item.description: Evalora has been added to PyPI, providing a simple way to evaluate AI code with just four lines of code. This tool simplifies the process of integrating evaluations into AI projects.

item.title: A “diff” tool for AI: Finding behavioral differences in new models
item.description: A new 'diff' tool for AI models has been developed to identify behavioral differences between versions. This tool enhances the evaluation process by providing deeper insights into model performance and safety.

item.title: Show HN: ACE – A dynamic benchmark measuring the cost to break AI agents
item.description: The ACE benchmark has been introduced to measure the cost of breaking AI agents, quantifying adversarial efforts in monetary terms. This allows for a more nuanced understanding of AI vulnerabilities.

item.title: zwischenzug 1.33.0
item.description: The Zwischenzug CLI has been released, featuring an AI coding agent powered by LangChain, designed to assist developers in coding tasks. This tool aims to improve productivity in software development.

item.title: conduit-ai 0.2.0
item.description: Conduit has launched version 0.2.0 of its Python SDK, which includes a LangChain retriever for knowledge graph integration. This update enhances the capabilities of developers working with knowledge graphs.

item.title: torchcurves 0.2.3
item.description: The torchcurves module has been updated to version 0.2.3, introducing differentiable parametric curves with learnable coefficients for PyTorch. This allows for more flexible modeling in machine learning.

item.title: euler-train 2.4.0
item.description: Euler-train has released version 2.4.0, a lightweight file-based experiment logger for PyTorch, aimed at simplifying the logging process for machine learning experiments.

item.title: turboquant-pytorch 0.1.2
item.description: Turboquant-pytorch 0.1.2 has been released, featuring a high-performance quantization engine with advanced techniques. This tool is designed to optimize model performance in PyTorch.

item.title: Groups Set Up to Shill AI and Data Centers Are Pouring Huge Sums of Money Into the Midterm Elections
item.description: Groups supporting AI and data centers are investing heavily in the midterm elections to influence policymakers. This financial backing aims to support those aligned with the current administration's tech agenda.

item.title: Best of the Genea-Blogs - Week of 29 March to 4 April 2026
item.description: This article provides a roundup of notable posts from genealogy blogs during the specified week. It highlights various topics and discussions within the genealogy community.

item.title: neuronx-sdk added to PyPI
item.description: The NeuronX AI Platform has released a Python SDK, offering 19 free LLMs and 185K patterns for developers. This tool aims to enhance accessibility to AI resources.

item.title: neuronx-sdk 0.1.0
item.description: NeuronX has launched version 0.1.0 of its Python SDK, featuring 19 free LLMs and 185K patterns. This release supports the development of autonomous agents.

item.title: IQVIA (IQV) Launches IQVIA.ai Unified Agentic AI Platform with Nvidia
item.description: IQVIA has introduced the IQVIA.ai Unified Agentic AI Platform in collaboration with Nvidia, aiming to enhance AI capabilities in healthcare analytics.

item.title: NCAA championship game predictions: AI picks winner of UConn vs Michigan
item.description: AI has predicted the winner of the NCAA championship game between UConn and Michigan, showcasing its capabilities in sports analytics.

item.title: Using Steve Little's AI Genealogy Research Assistant V8.5.1c To Develop a Research Plan
item.description: This article discusses using Steve Little's AI Genealogy Research Assistant to create effective research plans. It provides insights into optimizing genealogy research.

item.title: Navigating Recent Developments in Generative AI and Trade Secret Protection
item.description: Recent court decisions highlight risks associated with sharing confidential information with generative AI platforms. The cases emphasize the importance of trade secret protection.

item.title: bernstein 1.4.16
item.description: The article discusses version 1.4.16 of Bernstein, focusing on declarative agent orchestration for engineering teams. It aims to streamline engineering processes.

item.title: UPSC Quiz – 2026 : IASbaba’s Daily Current Affairs Quiz 4th April 2026
item.description: This quiz article presents current affairs questions based on reputable sources, aimed at preparing candidates for the UPSC exams. It focuses on key concepts and facts.
